elements.title: Rainelle Krause
elements.contents: ... that in 2020, Rainelle Krause (pictured) sang the "Queen of the Night aria" while doing aerial silks upside down?

elements.title: a dead raccoon
elements.contents: ... that a dead raccoon was honoured with a Toronto heritage plaque?

elements.title: Helene Pons
elements.contents: ... that Tony Award–nominated costume designer Helene Pons patented the first commercial underwire bra?

elements.title: endometriosis
elements.contents: ... that some women with endometriosis bleed from their lungs during their period?

elements.title: Mitchell's Causeway
elements.contents: ... that gangs of convict workers built Mitchell's Causeway by hand almost 200 years ago?

elements.title: David Rankin
elements.contents: ... that astronomer David Rankin has an asteroid, a comet, and a plesiosaur named after him?

elements.title: American Society of Marine Engineers
elements.contents: ... that the American Society of Marine Engineers was a company union established to supplant its labor union counterpart?

elements.title: a Byzantine detachment captured Verona
elements.contents: ... that a Byzantine detachment captured Verona during the night but became trapped as the defenders returned the following morning, and the soldiers had to jump from the city walls to escape?

elements.title: Sky Valley Mistress
elements.contents: ... that the rock duo Sky Valley Mistress tour in a hearse?
